description: Add SQLite-based persistence layer to the server for saving game state snapshots. Each game gets a record with metadata (id, name, players, status, timestamps). Every time the game state changes (broadcastState), a snapshot is saved. This enables resuming games and replaying them.
steps: Install better-sqlite3 and @types/better-sqlite3 as dependencies (npm install better-sqlite3 @types/better-sqlite3)

Create src/server/database.ts with: (a) initDatabase() function that creates/opens a SQLite DB file at data/games.db (relative to project root), creating the data/ directory if needed. (b) Tables: 'games' (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT, players_json TEXT, status TEXT ['active','finished'], created_at TEXT, updated_at TEXT, mode TEXT, winner TEXT), 'snapshots' (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT, game_id TEXT REFERENCES games(id), snapshot_index INTEGER, state_json TEXT, description TEXT, phase TEXT, season TEXT, created_at TEXT). (c) Export helper functions: saveGame(id, name, players, mode, status), updateGameStatus(id, status, winner?), saveSnapshot(gameId, state, description), getGameById(id), getActiveGames(), getFinishedGames(), getSnapshots(gameId), getSnapshotByIndex(gameId, index), getSnapshotCount(gameId)
In src/server/index.ts, import and call initDatabase() at server startup
Modify the START_GAME handler in src/server/index.ts to: save the new game to the DB using saveGame(), save the initial state as snapshot index 0

Modify the broadcastState() function in src/server/index.ts to also save a new snapshot each time state changes (increment snapshot_index). Include a description derived from the current phase/season. Also update the game's updated_at timestamp.
When a game reaches gameOver=true (detect in broadcastState), call updateGameStatus to mark as 'finished' with the winner
Add a Lobby interface field 'persistentGameId' to link the in-memory lobby to the database game record

Add REST endpoints: GET /api/games (list all games with status filter ?status=active|finished), GET /api/games/:id (get game metadata), GET /api/games/:id/snapshots (get all snapshots for replay), GET /api/games/:id/snapshots/:index (get specific snapshot), GET /api/games/:id/snapshot-count (get total snapshots)

Also support hotseat persistence: Add POST /api/games/save-hotseat endpoint that accepts a full GameState and saves it as a game + snapshot. Add PUT /api/games/:id/snapshot endpoint that accepts a GameState and appends a new snapshot for an existing game.
Add data/ to .gitignore so the SQLite file is not committed
acceptance_criteria: npm run build completes without TypeScript errors
Server starts successfully with npm run server (no crashes)
SQLite database file is created at data/games.db on first server start
GET /api/games returns an empty array initially
The database schema supports both active and finished game persistence with snapshot history
verification: Run npm run build and confirm it passes
Start server with timeout 5s and verify it logs startup message without errors
findings: Pre-existing TypeScript build errors in src/components/ActionPanel.tsx (unused variables betrayMonsterSelectionProvinceId and betrayMonsterSelectionFigureId) cause `npm run build` to fail. These are NOT caused by this feature. Server code runs fine via tsx. The `npm run build` command only builds the frontend (tsconfig.app.json excludes src/server). Server verified to start correctly and create data/games.db. All REST endpoints respond as expected.
